report_name: CISA Issues Urgent Advisory as 'FortiBleed' Campaign Compromises Over 86,000 Fortinet Devices Globally

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Russian-speaking threat group
threat_actors.description: A Russian-speaking threat group responsible for the FortiBleed campaign, which compromised over 86,000 Fortinet devices by cracking credentials collected from data breaches and infostealer logs.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Attackers used valid credentials, obtained through password cracking, to log into FortiGate SSL VPN portals and administrative interfaces. 35% of compromised accounts were generic 'admin' accounts.

attack_patterns.name: Brute Force
attack_patterns.description: Attackers used a 45-GPU cluster with Hashtopolis to crack password hashes collected from various sources. This includes both credential stuffing and password cracking against weak passwords.

attack_patterns.name: Protocol Tunneling
attack_patterns.description: Post-compromise, attackers used Chisel and Neo-reGeorg to tunnel traffic and pivot into internal networks.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Services
attack_patterns.description: The established tunnels were used to facilitate lateral movement into internal corporate networks via remote services.

attack_patterns.name: System Information Discovery
attack_patterns.description: Attackers may use the 'diagnose sys session list' command to view active sessions on a compromised FortiGate device.

tools.name: Hashtopolis
tools.description: Used to manage a 45-GPU cluster for cracking password hashes of Fortinet device credentials.

tools.name: Chisel
tools.description: Used as a post-exploitation tool for tunneling traffic to facilitate lateral movement.

tools.name: Neo-reGeorg
tools.description: Used as a post-exploitation tool for creating SOCKS proxies and tunneling traffic to pivot into internal networks.

campaigns.name: FortiBleed
campaigns.description: A massive credential harvesting and cracking campaign targeting Fortinet FortiGate firewalls and SSL VPN gateways, exploiting weak and default credentials.

identities.name: Telecommunications Sector
identities.description: One of the most affected sectors targeted by the FortiBleed campaign.

identities.name: Government Sector
identities.description: One of the most affected sectors targeted by the FortiBleed campaign.

identities.name: Education Sector
identities.description: One of the most affected sectors targeted by the FortiBleed campaign.

infrastructure.name: GPU Cracking Cluster
infrastructure.description: A 45-GPU cluster managed with Hashtopolis, used by the threat actor to crack password hashes of Fortinet device credentials.

indicators.name: Chisel or Neo-reGeorg File Indicator
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Chisel or Neo-reGeorg File Creation
id: 2b1c8d3e-4f5a-4b6c-8d9e-0f1a2b3c4d5e
status: experimental
description: Detects the presence of Chisel or Neo-reGeorg tunneling tool files.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/cisa-warns-fortinet-users-to-secure-devices-after-fortibleed-leak/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-20
tags:
  - attack.t1572
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\chisel.exe'
      - '/chisel'
      - '\neoregeorg.exe'
      - '/neoregeorg'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the presence of Chisel or Neo-reGeorg files, which are used for traffic tunneling in the FortiBleed campaign.

indicators.name: Chisel Tunneling Tool Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Chisel Tunneling Tool Execution
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-4a3b-2c1d-0e9f8a7b6c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of Chisel, a tool used for tunneling traffic over HTTP, commonly used for C2 and lateral movement.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/cisa-warns-fortinet-users-to-secure-devices-after-fortibleed-leak/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-20
tags:
  - attack.t1572
  - attack.command_and_control
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '/chisel'
      - '\chisel.exe'
    OriginalFileName: 'chisel.exe'
  condition: 1 of selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the Chisel tunneling tool, used for post-exploitation in the FortiBleed campaign.

indicators.name: Neo-reGeorg Tunneling Tool Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Neo-reGeorg Tunneling Tool Execution
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of Neo-reGeorg, a Python tool used for creating SOCKS proxies and tunneling traffic.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/cisa-warns-fortinet-users-to-secure-devices-after-fortibleed-leak/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-20
tags:
  - attack.t1572
  - attack.command_and_control
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains: 'neoregeorg'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the Neo-reGeorg tunneling tool, used for post-exploitation in the FortiBleed campaign.

indicators.name: FortiGate Session List Command Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: FortiGate Session List Command Execution
id: f0e9d8c7-b6a5-4b3c-2d1e-0f9e8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'diagnose sys session list' on a FortiGate device. Attackers may use this command to view active sessions.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/cisa-warns-fortinet-users-to-secure-devices-after-fortibleed-leak/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-20
tags:
  - attack.t1082
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains: 'diagnose sys session list'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of a FortiGate command used by attackers to discover active sessions on a compromised device.
